text:
From: Tom Ellis <t.ellis@carelink.health>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: CareLink Ops <ops@carelink.health>
Date: Fri, 20 Mar 2026 07:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Production errors — 429 flood since last night

Ava / team,

We're seeing a sharp spike in 429s starting ~01:30 UTC this morning after we moved a large cohort of traffic to the new enterprise tier. Impact: API calls from our ingestion pipeline are being rate-limited, causing downstream processing delays and timeouts for clinical syncs.

Quick details:
- Affected endpoints: /v1/generate and /v1/embeddings
- Sample org/project IDs: org_carelink_7 / proj-ingest-14
- Approx. 20% of requests returned 429, many followed by retries that compounding failures

Can you confirm whether any quota/tiering change rolled out today and whether you can temporarily raise limits while we stabilize? We're prepared to open a maintenance win...

- Baseline commit: 2 x A100-80GB equivalents (continuous), handles ~120 qps at 8k context with batching profile A.
- Burst pool: +1 GPU to absorb 2x short spikes (auto-burst).
- Throughput guarantee: 99th pct latency SLO target of 200 ms on the primary model + autoscaling policy.

Hi Ava,

text:
From: Ethan Ross <ethan.ross@orchid.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Orchid AI Procurement <procurement@orchid.ai>
Date: Tue, 27 May 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: SLA template: uptime anchoring & latency carveouts (Orchid AI)

Hi Ava,

Thanks for the demo last week — the platform looks great. Procurement reviewed the standard SLA you shared and we have two asks before we can move to PO: 

1) Uptime anchoring. For the first 90 days of the pilot we need a provisional uptime floor at 98.0% (rather than the standard 99.5%) due to the expected burst patterns during onboarding. We understand this is temporary and would expect the baseline to re-anchor at 99.5% after the pilot.

2) Latency carveouts. Our inference workload will use scheduled large-batch jobs overnight. We need explicit carveouts for batched inference windows (daily 0100–0400 PT) so batch-induced tail latency doesn’t...

Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 21, 2025 at 8:17 AM PT
Subject: Quantization Profiles: risk review + kill-switch proposal for beta

Ava,

Ahead of next week’s beta enablement with the design partners, we did a quick risk review on the Quantization Profile Library rollout path.

Top risks we want to explicitly guard:
1) Quality regressions (offline evals pass but edge-case prompts regress).
2) Incompatibility (profile enabled on a route but traffic lands on unsupported GPU SKU / context length / model variant).
3) Control plane or registry load issues causing elevated 5xx (thundering herd on profile metadata fetch).

Proposal (what we need leadership approval on):

Date: Tue, Feb 18, 2025 at 4:12 PM
Subject: Re: Redwood Inference security review: retention & deletion timelines

Sa...
text:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samir Desai <samir.desai@northpeakbank.com>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 18, 2025 at 4:12 PM
Subject: Re: Redwood Inference security review: retention & deletion timelines

Samir answering inline below.

1) Prompts/outputs retention: For Hosted API and Dedicated, we do not persistently store prompts and model outputs by default.
2) Request logs retention: We retain operational request logs (metadata only) for 14 days by default.
3) Debug payload logging: If you explicitly enable it for troubleshooting, retention is 7 days.
4) Audit logs: retained for 12 months.
5) Deletion requests: once validated, we complete deletion within 30 days for data stored in Redwood-managed systems.

Happy to jump on a quick call if helpful.

 Ava

--
Ava Chen
Enterpr...

Subject: Sizing question: Dedicated capacity for Q3 pilot

Hi Ava —

Thanks for the demo last week. We want to scope a small Dedicated deployment for a July pilot...
text:
From: Liam O'Sullivan <liam@helioscope.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sana Rao <sana@helioscope.ai>
Date: Fri, 15 Mar 2024 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Sizing question: Dedicated capacity for Q3 pilot

Hi Ava —

Thanks for the demo last week. We want to scope a small Dedicated deployment for a July pilot (NDA done). Quick background: our inference pattern is a mix of short chat completions and a reranking endpoint used by our search service. Typical request payloads are ~40–80 tokens; p95 tends to skew towards longer multi-turn flows.

Traffic profile (weekdays):
- Peak concurrent requests (observed): ~60 QPS on search rerank windows (bursty, 30s peaks).
- Average chat QPS outside peaks: 12–18.
- Average tokens per request: 55.
- 95th percentile tokens per request during peak windows: 140.

We care about p95 tail latency <= 300 ms for search rerank (single-turn) and < 700 ...
